data.title: Harvey AI boosts productivity by 35%, saving 7-10 hours per lawyer weekly. Legal tech firm eyes $11B valuation with $200M fundraising.
data.description: Case studies on the use of Harvey AI have demonstrated a weekly savings of 7-10 hours per lawyer. The legal AI startup is currently in the process of raising $200 million at a valuation of $11 billion, following the achievement of $190 million in annual recurring revenue. For further details, refer to the full article.

data.title: After losing in Supreme Court, Trump to implement tariffs in new way. Admin ready with plans for months.
data.description: For several months, the administration has been getting ready for the scenario in which the court might decide against the president and has devised alternate plans accordingly.

data.title: AMTA pushes for statewide massage therapy licensure in Minnesota (HF 362/SF 1131) to ensure public safety.
data.description: Advocate for the passage of HF 362/SF 1131 to enhance public safety and elevate the massage industry in Minnesota. On Feb. 20, 2026, in Evanston, the American Massage Therapy Association® (AMTA) disclosed plans to spearhead initiatives for implementing comprehensive massage therapy licensing across the state. This move aims to regulate the profession, ensuring adherence to standards and best practices. The proposal has garnered support from various stakeholders and is anticipated to set a precedent, benefiting an estimated (insert number) massage therapists practicing in Minnesota.

data.title: InventHelp inventor creates garment for coverage support for daughter, no straps or elastic; article from Pittsburgh, Feb. 20, 2026.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 20, 2026, an inventor shared plans to create a new garment for their autistic and schizoaffective daughter who refuses to wear a bra. The innovative design aims to offer support and coverage without the constraints of straps and elastic commonly found in bras. This new concept could potentially help individuals in similar circumstances, offering a more comfortable alternative.

data.title: Hilco Global is seeking offers to acquire IP assets of Filene's Basement, Running of the Brides, and Stanley Blacker, with indications due by March 18, 2026.
data.description: The deadline for submitting Indications of Interest is March 18, 2026 in New York City. A notable company, Hilco Global Professional Services, has been tasked with overseeing the sale of the intellectual property assets linked to three renowned American retail and fashion labels. The brands in question include Filene's. Stay updated at www.hilcoglobal.com.

data.title: Newmont Corp. (NYSE: NEM) shares drop after reporting earnings of $2.52 per share and revenue of $6.82 billion, beating estimates.
data.description: Newmont Corporation (NEM) reported fourth-quarter results, with earnings of $2.52 per share surpassing the estimate of $2, and revenue of $6.82 billion exceeding the expected $6.16 billion. The company's attributable gold production increased by 2% to 1,453,000 ounces, driven by new low-cost ounces at Ahafo North, higher grades at Tanami and Merian, and increased production from the Nevada Gold Mines joint venture. Newmont projects a total attributable production of 5.3 million ounces in 2026, with all-in sustaining costs of about $1,680 per ounce and a $4,500 per ounce gold price assumption. The company expects $1.950 billion in sustaining capital. As a result, Newmont shares are trading lower on the stock market.

data.title: Law firm Hagens Berman probes Ardent Health (ARDT) for $97M accounting shock; investors with losses urged to act.
data.description: Investors who suffered losses are advised to get in touch with the firm based in San Francisco. On February 20, 2026, the national law firm specializing in shareholder rights, Hagens Berman, issued a notice to investors involved with Ardent Health, Inc. (NYSE: ARDT) concerning the lead plaintiff deadline scheduled for March 9, 2026, in an ongoing securities investigation.

data.title: Zum to hold hiring fair for Shawnee Mission School District; awarded 7-year contract starting 2026, 3-day event for drivers/attendants.
data.description: A company based in Redwood City, California, Zūm, has secured a significant seven-year student transportation contract with the Shawnee Mission School District (SMSD) starting in the upcoming 2026-27 school year. To accommodate this contract, Zūm will be conducting a three-day hiring event aiming to recruit school bus drivers and attendants.

data.title: National nonprofit OHF partners with The Amelia Concours event, held March 5-8 by Hagerty, to support US Special Ops Forces.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida on February 20, 2026, Operation Healing Forces (OHF), a national nonprofit supporting the US Special Operations Forces community, revealed a significant partnership with The Amelia Concours. The event, in collaboration with Hagerty, is scheduled for March 5-8. OHF aims to benefit from this collaboration to further their mission and extend their outreach to a wider audience.
